en.extract:
The Arabian golden sparrow (Passer euchlorus) is a sparrow found in south west Arabia and also the coast of Somalia and Djibouti where it occurs in thorn savannah and scrub habitats. It is sometimes considered as a subspecies of the Sudan golden sparrow (Passer luteus).
It is sometimes kept as a pet.

Arabian golden sparrow at the Internet Bird Collection

[[File:Passer euchlorus, Petronia flavigula.jpg|thumb|right|An illustration of an Arabian golden sparrow (above) and a [[yellow-throated petronia]] (below) by [[Henrik Grönvold]]]]

The '''Arabian golden sparrow''' ('''''Passer euchlorus''''') is a [[Old World sparrow|sparrow]] found in south west [[Arabia]] and also the coast of Somalia and [[Djibouti]] where it occurs in thorn [[savannah]] and [[scrubland|scrub]] habitats. It is sometimes considered as a subspecies of the [[Sudan golden sparrow]] (''Passer luteus'').

It is sometimes kept as a pet.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Alshamlih |first1=Mohammed |last2=Alzayer |first2=Mohamed |last3=Hajwal |first3=Faisal |last4=Khalili |first4=May |last5=Khoury |first5=Fares |date=January 2022 |title=Introduced birds of Saudi Arabia: Status and potential impacts |journal=Journal of King Saud University - Science |volume=34 |issue=1 |article-number=101651 |doi=10.1016/j.jksus.2021.101651 |issn=1018-3647|doi-access=free }}</ref>
